mnemonics.title: Remember ARM Modes
mnemonics.content: User Fairy IRQs Supplicates All Unusual Demands - U(ser), FIQ, IRQ, S(upervisor), A(bort), U(ndefined), S(ystem)

mnemonics.title: Remember Vector Order
mnemonics.content: Rested Dogs Frighten Innocent People Unknowingly - Reset, Data Abort, FIQ, IRQ, Prefetch Abort, Undefined, SWI

mnemonics.title: Remember CPSR Bits Position
mnemonics.content: N Z C V I F T MMMMM - N(egative), Z(ero), C(arry), V(Overflow), I(RQ disable), F(IQ disable), T(humb), M(Mode bits)

acronyms.usage: Contains condition flags (N,Z,C,V), interrupt disable bits (I,F), T bit, and mode bits. Updated on every instruction execution.
Banked register that stores CPSR when an exception occurs. One SPSR for each privileged mode.
Stores return address when subroutine call or exception occurs. Different banked versions: LR_svc, LR_irq, LR_fiq, LR_abt, LR_und.
Points to top of stack. Banked across modes: SP_user, SP_svc, SP_irq, SP_fiq, SP_abt, SP_und for separate stack spaces.
High-priority interrupt with dedicated registers R8-R12, allowing faster interrupt handling with minimal register saving.
Normal priority interrupt, more common than FIQ. Requires saving more registers, hence slower than FIQ.
Instruction to trigger supervisor call for operating system services. Also called SVC (Supervisor Call) in ARMv7.

visualTips.visualization: Think of it as a doorbell system - each button (exception type) has a specific house address (handler location). Reset is at house 0, SWI at house 8, etc.
Like changing rooms in a house - each room (mode) has its own furniture (banked registers: SP, LR, SPSR). When you enter a room, you see only that room's furniture.
CPSR is like an aircraft cockpit - flags show status (N,Z,C,V), switches control interrupts (I,F), mode selector chooses flight mode (M bits).
Like catching a ball - processor catches exception (saves state to LR, SPSR), finds where to take it (vector table), handles it, then throws ball back (restores from LR, SPSR to return).

analogies.analogy: FIQ is like a dedicated express lane at a theme park - fewer people use it, faster service. IRQ is like the regular line - more common but slower.
Banked registers are like different bathrooms in a house - each family member has their own, they don't need to clean up before someone else uses theirs.

analogies.analization: The vector table is like an emergency contact list - each person (exception) has a specific phone number (handler address) to call when they need help.

analogies.analogy: SPSR is like a snapshot of your car's dashboard before taking it to a mechanic - the mechanic (exception handler) can see what was happening, then restores it when done.
Exception priority is like hospital triage - cardiac arrest (Reset) is most urgent, followed by severe injury (Data Abort), then serious injury (FIQ), then moderate injury (IRQ).
